title: Pithecellobium larensis
taxonomy: Pithecellobium larensis is classified as a species within the genus Pithecellobium, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. This classification places it among the legume family. The genus Pithecellobium is known to be comprised of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs. Pithecellobium larensis is native to the Americas, specifically in regions with warm and humid climates. The classification of Pithecellobium larensis is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.

properties: Pithecellobium larensis has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. preparations: Pithecellobium larensis is a plant species used in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Pithecellobium larensis are often used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. The plant's extracts are typically obtained through a process of drying and grinding the leaves and stems. intro: Pithecellobium larensis, also known as "Cachiman" or "Tepescohuite", is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pithecellobium larensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions, as well as oral supplements in the form of capsules and teas for general health and well-being.
